extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won a millimetric sprint over GROENEWEGEN Dylan after mechanical disruption in final corner, completing a hat-trick of stage wins.
tactical_patterns: BENNETT Sam positioned front at 60km to avoid crashes despite energy cost, prioritizing safety over conservation in wet conditions.
Break sharing bonus seconds equally neutralizes strategic value; riders must commit to organized distribution or aggressive solo theft.
PHILIPSEN Jasper leading out without sprint train forces GROENEWEGEN to improvise right-side attack, creating vulnerability to isolated counter-punch.
Final corner positioning decisive when mechanical issues prevent throwing bike; BENNETT won via momentum draft off UAE rider, not power.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: winning despite broken gears and skipping chain shows exceptional bike handling under mechanical duress in sprint chaos
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: matching BENNETT's speed without lead-out train demonstrates raw power but lost millimetric finish suggests tactical positioning deficit
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: third place without dedicated train reveals emerging ability to self-position in disorganized finale
rider_signals.ROELANDTS Jürgen: positive: fourth place plus bonus seconds collection across stage shows consistent top-5 finishing speed without team support
rider_signals.TANFIELD Harry: neutral: contested intermediate sprints aggressively in break but failed to coordinate bonus second strategy with companions
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: team actively sheltering him from crashes suggests targeting different objective than sprint stages
rider_signals.MEURISSE Xandro: negative: crash involvement appeared to end his race participation early, suggesting vulnerability in wet technical sections
